LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE ISSUE OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO SECTION 20(C) OF THE AGREEMENT (COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE.) INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS COMPENSATED FOR GOODWILL. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES TIPS IN HIS FAVOR. MOTION DENIED AS TO SECTION 20(D) OF THE AGREEMENT (EMPLOYEE NO-HIRE PROVISION.) THE LAW ALLOWS PLAINTIFF TO HIRE EMPLOYEES IF THEY APPROACH HIM ON THEIR OWN. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN HE WILL SUFFER IRREPARABLE HARM IF HE IS NOT ABLE TO HIRE C.C.S. EMPLOYEES, AND THE MOTION IS DENIED ON THAT BASIS. AT THIS TIME, NO BOND WILL BE REQUIRED. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE AND CONTINUES THE MATTER TO SEPTEMBER 14, 2010. DEFENDANTS MAY FILE A FURTHER OPPOSITION (TO BE FILED BY SEPTEMBER 8, 2010) TO ADDRESS ISSUES RAISED IN THE REPLY. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A REPLY (WITH NO ADDITIONAL EVIDENCE) BY SEPTEMBER 10, 2010. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER GRANTING CONTINUANCE OF DATE OF HEARING/OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

LAW AND MOTION, 302 PLAINTIFF LEV WEISBACH'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE ISSUANCE OF/MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: THERE HAS BEEN A DEMONSTRATION THAT THE HARDSHIP TIPS IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANTS. COURT DOES NOT AGREE WITH THE GOODWILL. HOWEVER LOOKING AT THIS PRESENTATION, AT THE FIRM AND WHAT THE EVIDENCE IS AND THE DECLARATION, THE HARDSHIP TIPS IN FAVOR OF THE DEFENDANTS. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION ON THAT BASIS ALONE. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff'S Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-28-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF LEV WEISBACH'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE ISSUANCE OF/MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

APPEARANCES: Maureen E, O'Neil, Esq. 489-8264 for pltf & moving party; Jason A. Geller, Esq. of Long & Levit LLP 397-2222 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court ruled as follows: There has been a demonstration that the hardship tips in favor of the defendants. Court does not agree with the goodwill. However looking at this presentation, at the firm and what the evidence is and the declaration, the hardship tips in favor of the defendants. The Court denies the motion on that basis alone. (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Plaintiff'S Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-02-2010 Continued To Sep-28-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Plaintiff'S Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Aug-26-2010 Continued To Sep-14-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE ISSUE OF PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. Appearances: Maureen E. O'Neill of Paul Hastings (650.320.1811) for plaintiff and moving party; Jason A. Geller of Long & Levit LLP (397.2222) for defendant and responding parties. Argued and the court modified its ruling as follows: motion granted in part and denied in part. Motion granted as to section 20(c) of the Agreement (covenant not to compete.) Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff was compensated for goodwill. Plaintiff has shown a likelihood of success on the merits and the balance of equities tips in his favor. Motion denied as to section 20(D) of the Agreement (employee no-hire provision.) The law allows plaintiff to hire employees if they approach him on their own. However, plaintiff has not shown he will suffer irreparable harm if he is not able to hire C.C.S. employees, and the motion is denied on that basis. At this time, no bond will be required. The court grants defendants' request for a continuance and continues the matter to September 14, 2010. Defendants may file a further opposition (to be filed by September 8, 2010) to address issues raised in the reply. Plaintiff may file a reply (with no additional evidence) by September 10, 2010. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
